backlog.title: Bugfix Round 2 — Completion, Discovery Safety, and CLI Consistency
backlog.description: Fix two Major issues (--shell completion value routing; vanished configured store silently falling through) and three Minor issues (inconsistent missing-value exit codes for --search/--shell; POSIX -- separator rejected; README version doc inaccuracy). All five issues are explicitly specified in the PRD and validated against the current codebase. The changes touch completion files, internal/skillsdir, main.go parseArgs/run, and README.md.

milestones.title: Major Issues (Should Fix)
milestones.description: Issue 1: --shell value completion offers skill tags instead of bash/zsh/fish. Issue 2: a configured store whose directory has vanished silently falls through to a different store (wrong skills loaded, undermines the pi --skill contract).

tasks.title: Fix --shell value completion to offer the bash/zsh/fish enum (Issue 1)
tasks.description: All three shell completion files (bash, zsh, fish) are missing --shell value routing. After --shell, tab-completion offers skill tags instead of the fixed enum bash/zsh/fish. Each file needs a --shell value-completion directive added. The files are embedded via //go:embed so they must be modified on disk and rebuilt; TestEmbeddedCompletionsMatchOnDisk verifies byte-identity automatically.

subtasks.title: Add --shell value routing and flag advertisement to bash completion file
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/bugfix/001_ba6f35f74a59/architecture/issue_analysis.md §Issue 1 and decisions.md §D7. The file is completions/skilldozer.bash. It is embedded via //go:embed at main.go (var bashCompletion). The file has a `case "$prev" in` block (around line 38) with cases for --search (return 0 — offer nothing) and --store|--init (compgen -d — directory completion). There is NO --shell case. The flag advertisement list at ~line 44 uses `compgen -W "--version --help --path --list --all --file --relative --no-color --search --store --check --init --completions"` (13 flags, no --shell).
2. INPUT: Read completions/skilldozer.bash in full first to confirm exact line numbers and surrounding context.
3. LOGIC: (a) In the `case "$prev" in` block, add a new case AFTER the `--store|--init)` line: `--shell) COMPREPLY=($(compgen -W "bash zsh fish" -- "$cur")); return 0 ;;`. This routes the --shell value slot to the fixed enum. (b) Add `--shell` to the compgen -W flag list string at ~line 44 so `skilldozer --<TAB>` advertises it (decision D7: --shell is a real documented flag in usageText, so it should be discoverable). Keep the LOCKSTEP comment header intact and accurate.
4. OUTPUT: Modified completions/skilldozer.bash. After `go build`, the embedded bytes match the on-disk file (TestEmbeddedCompletionsMatchOnDisk passes). The repro `bash -c 'eval "$(./skilldozer --completions --shell bash)"; COMP_WORDS=(skilldozer --shell ""); COMP_CWORD=2; _skilldozer_completion; echo "COMPREPLY=[${COMPREPLY[*]}]"'` yields COMPREPLY=[bash zsh fish].
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The completion file's header comment already documents the LOCKSTEP constraint. Verify it still mentions all flags including --shell. No external doc change needed — the change is internal to the completion script.

subtasks.title: Add --shell value entry and flag advertisement to zsh completion file
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/bugfix/001_ba6f35f74a59/architecture/issue_analysis.md §Issue 1 and decisions.md §D7. The file is completions/_skilldozer. It is embedded via //go:embed at main.go (var zshCompletion). The file defines a `local -a flags=( ... )` array with _arguments entries. Existing value-taking entries use the pattern `'--search[Description]:query:'` (free-text) and `'--store[Description]:directory:_files'` (path completion). There is NO --shell entry. The `*: :->args` catch-all at the end routes unmatched positional slots to tag completion.
2. INPUT: Read completions/_skilldozer in full first to confirm exact line numbers and surrounding context.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add a new entry to the `flags=( ... )` array for --shell, placed after the --completions entry: `'--shell[Force a shell for completion]:shell:(bash zsh fish)'`. The `:shell:(bash zsh fish)` syntax tells _arguments that the value slot offers the three words in the parenthesized list. (b) Keep the LOCKSTEP comment header intact. No other changes needed — _arguments handles the routing automatically once the entry is in the array.
4. OUTPUT: Modified completions/_skilldozer. After `go build`, the embedded bytes match (TestEmbeddedCompletionsMatchOnDisk passes). In zsh, `skilldozer --shell <TAB>` offers bash, zsh, fish (not skill tags).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The completion file's header comment already documents the LOCKSTEP constraint. Verify the flag list comment mentions --shell. No external doc change needed.

subtasks.title: Add --shell value directive and flag advertisement to fish completion file
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/bugfix/001_ba6f35f74a59/architecture/issue_analysis.md §Issue 1 and decisions.md §D7. The file is completions/skilldozer.fish. It is embedded via //go:embed at main.go (var fishCompletion). The file uses individual `complete -c skilldozer -l <flag> -d '<desc>'` directives. Value-taking flags like --store use `-r` (require a value, route to file completion). --search deliberately does NOT use `-r` (free-text → offer nothing). There is NO --shell directive. The dynamic tag completion directive at the bottom fires for unmatched positionals.
2. INPUT: Read completions/skilldozer.fish in full first to confirm exact line numbers.
3. LOGIC: Add a new directive after the --store line: `complete -c skilldozer -l shell -d 'Force a shell for completion' -x -a "bash zsh fish"`. The `-x` flag means "no file completion" (exclusive), and `-a "bash zsh fish"` provides the three enum values. This is the INVERSE of --store's `-r` (which routes to file completion): --shell offers a fixed enum, not files. Keep the LOCKSTEP comment header intact.
4. OUTPUT: Modified completions/skilldozer.fish. After `go build`, the embedded bytes match (TestEmbeddedCompletionsMatchOnDisk passes). In fish, `skilldozer --shell <TAB>` offers bash, zsh, fish (not skill tags).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The completion file's header comment already documents the LOCKSTEP constraint. Verify the flag list comment mentions --shell. No external doc change needed.

tasks.title: Error loudly when a configured store directory has vanished (Issue 2)
tasks.description: When the config file is present and names a store: that does not exist on disk, findConfig() currently treats this as a miss and silently falls through to the sibling-of-binary / walk-up rules. If one of those exists, skilldozer resolves tags from an unrelated store with exit 0 and no warning. The fix distinguishes 'config present but store vanished' from 'config missing/unreadable' and returns a distinct error so pi --skill fails loudly.

subtasks.title: Distinguish vanished-store from unconfigured in findConfig and propagate through Find
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/bugfix/001_ba6f35f74a59/architecture/issue_analysis.md §Issue 2, decisions.md §D1-D3, and research/skillsdir_analysis.md. The current findConfig() signature is `func findConfig() (dir string, src Source, found bool)` at skillsdir.go:106. It has 5 return paths (lines 109, 113, 116, 126, 128) — all misses return `("", 0, false)`. Line 126 is the bug: `return "", 0, false // store path is not an existing dir -> fall through`. Find() at line 292 calls `findConfig()` and only checks the `found` bool. findConfig is called from exactly ONE production site (Find, line 292) and 6 test sites (skillsdir_test.go:553-624). The sentinel pattern follows ErrNotFound (skillsdir.go:283). All main.go callers already handle err != nil via `fmt.Fprintln(stderr, err); return 1` — NO main.go change is needed. The `unsetEnvVar` test helper points SKILLDOZER_CONFIG at a non-existent FILE, so config.Load returns fs.ErrNotExist → findConfig bails at line 113 (not 126) → TestFindAllMissReturnsErrNotFound is SAFE (vanishedStore stays "").
2. INPUT: The existing findConfig() function at internal/skillsdir/skillsdir.go:106-128 and Find() at :288-302. The store path is already computed (variable `store` at line 118-123) before the os.Stat check at line 124.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add a package-level sentinel error near ErrNotFound: `var ErrConfiguredStoreMissing = errors.New("configured skills store directory does not exist")`. (b) Change findConfig() signature to `func findConfig() (dir string, src Source, found bool, vanishedStore string)`. At line 126, change `return "", 0, false` to `return "", 0, false, store` (store is the resolved path that doesn't exist). At ALL other return paths (lines 109, 113, 116, 128), append `, ""`. (c) In Find() at line 292-294, change to: `d, s, ok, vanished := findConfig()` then `if ok { return d, s, nil }` then `if vanished != "" { return "", 0, fmt.Errorf("%w: configured store %q does not exist; run `skilldozer --init` or recreate the directory", ErrConfiguredStoreMissing, vanished) }`. (d) Update all 6 existing findConfig test call sites to accept the 4th return value (append `, _` or `, vanished` as appropriate). (e) Update TestFindConfigStoreDirAbsent (skillsdir_test.go:587): it must now assert `found == false AND vanishedStore != ""` (the store path). Rename to TestFindConfigStoreVanished for clarity. (f) Add new test TestFindErrorsOnVanishedConfiguredStore: write a valid config with a non-existent store dir, call Find() (not findConfig), assert errors.Is(err, ErrConfiguredStoreMissing) and the message contains the configured path + 'skilldozer --init'. (g) Add new test TestFindEnvOverridesVanishedStore: set SKILLDOZER_SKILLS_DIR to an existing dir AND config with vanished store → env wins → no error.
4. OUTPUT: Modified internal/skillsdir/skillsdir.go (findConfig + Find + sentinel) and internal/skillsdir/skillsdir_test.go (6 updated + 2 new tests). The error propagates to all main.go callers unchanged (they already do fmt.Fprintln(stderr, err); return 1). `go test ./internal/skillsdir/...` passes.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the findConfig doc comment at skillsdir.go:92-105 to document the new vanishedStore return value and the distinction between 'config missing → fall through' and 'store vanished → error'. Update the Find() doc comment at skillsdir.go:265-282 to document the new error path. No README or user-facing doc change needed (the error message IS the user-facing doc).

milestones.title: Minor Issues (Nice to Fix)
milestones.description: Issue 3: --search/--shell with no value print help (exit 0) while --store exits 2 — make them symmetrical. Issue 4: POSIX -- end-of-options separator is rejected as an unknown flag. Issue 5: README version documentation is inaccurate for plain go build. Issues 3 and 4 both modify parseArgs() in main.go and should be applied sequentially to avoid conflicts.

tasks.title: Make missing-value handling consistent for all value-taking flags (Issue 3)
tasks.description: --store (no value) exits 2 with an error, but --search (no value) and --shell (no value) print help to stdout and exit 0. This asymmetry means $(skilldozer --search) captures help text. Fix: treat missing --search/--shell values as parse errors (exit 2), mirroring --store.

subtasks.title: Add missing-value exit-2 detection for --search and --shell in parseArgs and run
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/bugfix/001_ba6f35f74a59/architecture/issue_analysis.md §Issue 3, decisions.md §D4-D5, and research/parseargs_analysis.md. The config struct is at main.go:153-170. The existing pattern is: `storeMissingValue bool` field (main.go:169) set in parseArgs when --store has no value (3 places: main.go:229 for --store=, :237 for --init=, :318 for bare --store last token), then checked in run() at main.go:499-502 (`if c.storeMissingValue { fmt.Fprintln(stderr, "skilldozer: --store requires a value"); return 2 }`). The --search case is at main.go:288-298 (case "--search", "-s": if i+1 < len(args) { ... } else { nothing } ). The --shell case is at main.go:320-326 (case "--shell": if i+1 < len(args) { ... } else { nothing }). The short-bundle -s no-value case is in expandShortBundle at main.go:444 (default: comment says searchMode stays false). The misleading comment is at main.go:293 saying '(exit 1)' but actual behavior is exit 0. The run() precedence ladder is: help(471) → version(477) → unknownFlag(484) → storeMissingValue(499) → exclusivity(508). The =-form for --search (main.go:218) has NO empty-value guard (decision D5: leave as-is, only the bare no-token case gets the fix).
2. INPUT: The config struct (main.go:153-170), parseArgs (main.go:182-358), expandShortBundle (main.go:361-450), and run() (main.go:466-504). The existing storeMissingValue pattern is the template.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add two fields to the config struct after storeMissingValue (main.go:169): `searchMissingValue bool  // --search/-s seen with NO following value` and `shellMissingValue bool  // --shell seen with NO following value`. (b) In parseArgs main switch case "--search", "-s": (main.go:296), add `else { c.searchMissingValue = true }` to the `if i+1 < len(args)` block. (c) In parseArgs main switch case "--shell": (main.go:325), add `else { c.shellMissingValue = true }`. (d) In expandShortBundle (main.go:444 default case), add `c.searchMissingValue = true` before the comment. (e) In run() AFTER the storeMissingValue check (main.go:502), add: `if c.searchMissingValue { fmt.Fprintln(stderr, "skilldozer: --search requires a query"); return 2 }` and `if c.shellMissingValue { fmt.Fprintln(stderr, "skilldozer: --shell requires a value (bash|zsh|fish)"); return 2 }`. (f) Fix the misleading comment at main.go:293: change 'falls through to the no-recognized-mode default (exit 1)' to 'records searchMissingValue so run() exits 2 with --search requires a query'. Also fix the comment at main.go:324 for --shell similarly.
4. OUTPUT: Modified main.go (config struct + 3 parseArgs branches + run() checks + comment fixes) and main_test.go (new tests). New tests: TestRunSearchNoValueExits2 (run([]string{"--search"}) → exit 2, stderr has 'requires a query', stdout empty), TestRunSearchShortNoValueExits2 (run([]string{"-s"}) → exit 2), TestRunShellNoValueExits2 (run([]string{"--shell"}) → exit 2, stderr has 'requires a value', stdout empty), TestParseArgsSearchMissingValue (parseArgs([]string{"--search"}).searchMissingValue == true), TestParseArgsShellMissingValue (parseArgs([]string{"--shell"}).shellMissingValue == true).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Fix the misleading inline comments at main.go:293 and main.go:324 to reflect the new exit-2 behavior. The --store precedent comment at main.go:493-498 should be updated to mention --search and --shell follow the same pattern. No README or external doc change needed (the error messages are self-documenting).

tasks.title: Support POSIX -- end-of-options separator (Issue 4)
tasks.description: A bare -- token is classified as an unknown dashed flag (exit 2). POSIX convention requires -- to mean 'all subsequent tokens are positional arguments', so a tag beginning with - can be resolved. Add a loop-level endOfOpts flag to parseArgs.

subtasks.title: Recognize -- as end-of-options separator in parseArgs
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/bugfix/001_ba6f35f74a59/architecture/issue_analysis.md §Issue 4 and decisions.md §D6. parseArgs is at main.go:182-358. The loop is `for i := 0; i < len(args); i++ { a := args[i]; ... }`. A bare -- currently: fails the =-form guard (main.go:202: no = in --), fails the short-bundle guard (main.go:259: len("--")==2, not > 2), reaches switch a → default (main.go:343), where strings.HasPrefix("--", "-") is true → c.unknownFlag = "--" → run() exits 2 with 'unknown flag --'. There is NO end-of-options semantics anywhere.
2. INPUT: The parseArgs function at main.go:182-358. The loop starts at main.go:190 (`for i := 0; i < len(args); i++ {`). The first token classification is the =-form check at main.go:202.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add `endOfOpts := false` before the for loop (after `var c config` at main.go:188). (b) Inside the loop, BEFORE the =-form check (main.go:202), add two guards: `if endOfOpts { c.tags = append(c.tags, a); continue }` (everything after -- is positional) and `if a == "--" { endOfOpts = true; continue }` (the -- token itself is consumed as the separator). These must be the FIRST checks in the loop body, before any other token classification. (c) Add a doc comment explaining the POSIX -- convention and why it matters for tags beginning with -.
4. OUTPUT: Modified main.go (parseArgs loop). New tests in main_test.go: TestParseArgsDashDashSeparator (parseArgs([]string{"--", "-x"}) → tags==["-x"], unknownFlag==""), TestParseArgsDashDashBeforeTag (parseArgs([]string{"--", "mytag"}) → tags==["mytag"]), TestParseArgsDashDashWithFlags (parseArgs([]string{"--list", "--", "--check"}) → list==true, tags==["--check"]), TestRunDashDashUnknownFlagStillWorks (run([]string{"--", "--bogus"}) → resolves --bogus as a tag, not unknown flag). Edge: `skilldozer -- --` → second -- is a positional tag named "--" (valid per POSIX).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add a brief inline comment in parseArgs documenting the POSIX -- convention. Optionally mention in README.md Usage section that -- can prefix tags starting with - (include in the final doc sweep task P1.M3.T1.S1).

tasks.title: Fix README version documentation accuracy (Issue 5)
tasks.description: The README states 'Version is the git-describe value (dynamic, not a fixed string)' but this is only true for the install.sh build. A plain go build (the README's own 'From source' path) yields 'dev'. Tighten the documentation to reflect reality.

subtasks.title: Clarify version string documentation in README
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/bugfix/001_ba6f35f74a59/architecture/issue_analysis.md §Issue 5. The README.md 'Usage' section contains the comment line: `# Version is the git-describe value (dynamic, not a fixed string)`. The version var is at main.go:57: `var version = "dev"`. The install.sh script injects `-ldflags "-X main.version=$(git describe --tags --always 2>/dev/null || echo dev)"` but a plain `go build -o skilldozer .` (README's 'From source' path C) does NOT, yielding `skilldozer dev`. The 'From source' section is at README.md around the `go build -o skilldozer .` instruction.
2. INPUT: README.md (read the Usage and Install sections to find the exact line and surrounding context).
3. LOGIC: Change the comment line from `# Version is the git-describe value (dynamic, not a fixed string)` to `# Version is the git-describe value when built via ./install.sh; a plain 'go build' reports 'dev'`. This accurately describes both build paths. Keep the line as a bash comment (prefixed with #) in the Usage examples block.
4. OUTPUT: Modified README.md with the corrected version documentation line.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] This subtask IS a documentation fix. The README change is the entire deliverable. No code changes.

milestones.title: Changeset-Level Documentation Sync
milestones.description: Cross-cutting documentation sweep to ensure the README, help text, and completion file headers are consistent after all five issues are fixed. This is the Mode B catch-all that prevents a coherent delta from shipping with a stale overview.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Verify README.md, usageText in main.go, and completion file headers are consistent with all changes from this bugfix round. Check that --shell is documented everywhere it should be, the error contract section reflects the new exit-2 behavior for --search/--shell, and the POSIX -- convention is mentioned if appropriate.

subtasks.title: Sweep README, help text, and completion headers for changeset consistency
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: This task runs AFTER all implementing subtasks are complete. It depends on every other subtask in this changeset. See plan/004_5851dcff4371/bugfix/001_ba6f35f74a59/architecture/ for the full issue analysis.
2. INPUT: The final state of all modified files after P1.M1 and P1.M2 subtasks complete: completions/skilldozer.bash, completions/_skilldozer, completions/skilldozer.fish (Issue 1), internal/skillsdir/skillsdir.go (Issue 2), main.go (Issues 3+4), README.md (Issue 5).
3. LOGIC: Perform a consistency sweep: (a) README.md Usage section: verify the Error contract paragraph mentions exit 2 for --search/--shell missing values (Issue 3) if it previously only mentioned --store. Verify the POSIX -- convention is mentioned if the Usage section discusses tag addressing (Issue 4) — this is optional since such tags are pathological. (b) main.go usageText: verify --shell is in the OPTIONS section (it already is at main.go:116) and the --completions line mentions [--shell <name>] (it already does). No change expected unless prior subtasks introduced drift. (c) completion file LOCKSTEP headers: verify all three files list --shell in their documented flag set (if they enumerate flags in comments). (d) README Install section: verify the version documentation from P1.M2.T3.S1 is consistent with the install.sh vs go build distinction.
4. OUTPUT: Any small doc fixes discovered during the sweep. If no inconsistencies are found, document that fact (the sweep is the deliverable — it confirms consistency).
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the changeset-level documentation task. Its purpose is to catch any cross-cutting doc drift that individual subtasks (Mode A) would not have addressed.
